dimensions.name: Prompt Completeness
dimensions.rationale: Identity >50 words. Instructions >200 words with 5 numbered steps. Tool guidance with Figma REST, Linear GraphQL, Slack API specs. Three examples (new components, no changes, removal). Error handling with Figma 401/429/404, Linear GraphQL errors, Slack errors, state corruption.

dimensions.name: Tool-Prompt Alignment
dimensions.rationale: http_request mapped to Figma REST v1, Linear GraphQL, Slack Web API. file_read/file_write for figma_state.json and handoff_log.json. No phantom tools.

dimensions.name: Trigger Coherence
dimensions.rationale: Webhook for real-time Figma FILE_VERSION_UPDATE events + 10-min polling as fallback. Both documented in prompt with appropriate use cases.

dimensions.name: Connector Coverage
dimensions.rationale: All 3 service_flow entries (Figma, Linear, Slack) have complete connector definitions with PAT/API key/bot token credential fields.

dimensions.name: Memory Design
dimensions.rationale: Local state files for version tracking (figma_state.json) and audit trail (handoff_log.json). Adequate for the use case.

dimensions.name: Error Handling
dimensions.rationale: Figma 401/429/404, Linear GraphQL authentication and timeout errors with pending operation recovery, Slack channel errors with fallback, state corruption handling with fresh-start baseline.

dimensions.name: Use Case Fidelity
dimensions.rationale: All 3 use_case_flows covered: new version handoff, error recovery and retry, first-run baseline initialization.

dimensions.name: Value Clarity
dimensions.rationale: Replaces 3 automation workflows. Bridges design-dev gap with component-level tracking, Linear ticket creation, and Slack summaries.

dimensions.name: Execution Feasibility
dimensions.rationale: Figma API, Linear GraphQL, Slack API are all standard. All connectors have builtin definitions. Polling and webhook triggers are actionable.

dimensions.name: Differentiation
dimensions.rationale: Component-level diffing with categorization (ADDED/MODIFIED/REMOVED), priority mapping based on change type, first-run baseline prevention of ticket flooding.

dimensions.name: Variable Necessity
dimensions.rationale: Adoption questions target config values that are genuinely needed: Figma file keys, Linear team ID, Slack channel, thread threshold.

dimensions.name: Default Quality
dimensions.rationale: Figma file keys and Linear team ID have empty defaults requiring mandatory user input. Slack channel (#design-handoffs) and thread threshold (10) have good defaults.

issues: No adoption_questions existed before fix - Figma file keys, Linear team ID, Slack channel needed user-specific configuration
Figma file keys and Linear team ID have no sensible defaults - user must provide before first run

template: Added 4 adoption_questions: figma_file_keys, linear_team_id, slack_design_channel, batch_thread_threshold
